mainEntity.name: Can I take professional product photos with an iPhone?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, you can take professional‑looking product photos with an iPhone if you control light, use a steady rig, and edit properly. The research shows that the two most advanced techniques , using Pro mode and shooting RAW , give results that rival a cheap DSLR. For very small jewellery or high‑gloss items you may still want a specialist, but most small‑scale e‑commerce work can be done with a phone.

mainEntity.name: What is the best lighting for product photos on an iPhone?

acceptedAnswer.text: The best lighting is soft, diffused light from a window or a softbox. Position the key light at a 45‑degree angle and use a white reflector or foam board opposite it to fill shadows. If natural light isn’t available, a LED panel with a diffuser works well. Over‑expose the background by one stop to keep it pure white.

mainEntity.name: What background works best for iPhone product photography?

acceptedAnswer.text: A clean white sweep or smooth paper is the safest choice. It removes distractions and makes colour correction easy. For lifestyle shots, a neutral coloured wall or a textured surface that matches the brand can add interest without stealing focus.

mainEntity.name: How do I make iPhone product photos look sharper?

acceptedAnswer.text: Sharpness comes from steady support and proper focus. Use a tripod, lock focus with a tap‑and‑hold, and turn off image stabilisation when the phone is mounted. Shoot at ISO 100‑200 and use a small aperture (f/8‑f/11) if your app lets you adjust it. After shooting, select the sharpest frame from a burst.

mainEntity.name: Can I photograph jewellery or reflective products with an iPhone?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can, but you need to manage reflections. Use a black card behind the piece, a polarising filter if you have one, and a diffused light source. Slightly tilt the product and adjust the angle of the light to keep hot spots down. Shooting in RAW helps you recover highlight detail later.

mainEntity.name: When should I hire a professional product photographer?

acceptedAnswer.text: If you need ultra‑high detail, consistent colour across many SKUs, or you’re shooting complex items like glassware, a professional photographer can save you time. Also, if you lack a dedicated space or need 360° views, hiring a pro is worth the cost.
